paper_metadata.title: BlenderBot 3: a deployed conversational agent that continually learns to responsibly engage
paper_metadata.abstract: We present BlenderBot 3, a 175B parameter dialogue model capable of open-domain conversation with access to the internet and a long-term memory, and having been trained on a large number of user defined tasks. We release both the model weights and code, and have also deployed the model on a public web page to interact with organic users. This technical report describes how the model was built (architecture, model and training scheme), and details of its deployment, including safety mechanisms. Human evaluations show its superiority to existing open-domain dialogue agents, including its predecessors (Roller et al., 2021; Komeili et al., 2022). Finally, we detail our plan for continual learning using the data collected from deployment, which will also be publicly released. The goal of this research program is thus to enable the community to study ever-improving responsible agents that learn through interaction.

extraction_data.innovation: BlenderBot 3 introduces a deployed conversational agent that continually learns from user interactions and incorporates safety mechanisms to recover from unsafe generations, while maintaining engaging dialogue.
extraction_data.finetuning_task: Multi-task fine-tuning on dialogue tasks including personality, empathy, long-term memory, knowledge, and safety
extraction_data.extension: DIRECTOR (Arora et al., 2022) employs a language model head and a classifier head at every step during left-right generation, predicting the next token by combining the two probabilities to improve dialogue responses.
extraction_data.research_problem: Continual learning of conversational agents with safety and engagement
extraction_data.paper_title: BlenderBot 3: a deployed conversational agent that continually learns to responsibly engage

extraction_data.innovation: Continual learning through human feedback mechanisms, including DIRECTOR for incorporating binary feedback to improve dialogue responses.
extraction_data.extension: DIRECTOR (Arora et al., 2022) employs a language model head and a classifier head at every step during left-right generation, predicting the next token by combining the two probabilities to improve dialogue responses.
extraction_data.research_problem: Continual learning of intelligent agents through interaction with humans and the world
extraction_data.application: Conversational agent that continually learns to responsibly engage
extraction_data.paper_title: BlenderBot 3: a deployed conversational agent that continually learns to responsibly engage
